ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF QUALITY INTERNATIONAL PACKAGING, INC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ADD CAUSES OF ACTION 3 THROUGH 7 AGAINST DEFENDANT WORLD OF CHARMS, INC. AND SASIMU LLC, SIMAYOF, INC., AND SIMUS INC. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO SARIT SIMAYOF-COHEN, ADI SIMAYOF, AND YEDIDIA SIMAYOF. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN OF THEIR POTENTIAL LIABILITY EARLIER AND ALLOWING AMENDMENT A MERE 4 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL. IN ADDITION, IF PLAINTIFF RECOVERS AGAINST DEFENDANT(S), PLAINTIFF CAN BRING A POST JUDGMENT MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 187 TO ADD THE INDIVIDUALS AS ADDITIONAL ALTER EGO JUDGMENT DEBTORS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF FEB-18-2015 CONTINUED TO APR-01-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. OVERRULED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR MONEY DUE, BOOK ACCOUNT, QUANTUM MERUIT, DECLARATORY RELIEF, AND FRAUDULENT TRANSFER. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS TO STATE THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION. THE SAC DOES NOT PLEAD FRAUD WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. IN ORDER TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AGAINST A CORPORATE DEFENDANT, THE COMPLAINT MUST ALLEGE WHO AT THE CORPORATION MADE SUCH STATEMENTS AND ALLEGE THEIR AUTHORITY TO SPEAK FOR THE CORPORATION. (LAZAR V. SUP. CT. (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUSTEE CAUSE OF ACTION. "A CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST IS NOT AN INDEPENDENT CAUSE OF ACTION BUT MERELY A TYPE OF REMEDY." (BATT V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (2007) 155 CAL.APP.4TH 65, 82.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WORLD OF CHARMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Overruled as to the causes of action for money due, book account, quantum meruit, declaratory relief, and fraudulent transfer. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads facts to state those causes of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. The SAC does not plead fraud with the requisite specificity. In order to state a cause of action for fraud against a corporate defendant, the complaint must allege who at the corporation made such statements and allege their authority to speak for the corporation. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Sustained without leave to amend as to the constructive trustee cause of action. "A constructive trust is not an independent cause of action but merely a type of remedy." (Batt v. City and County of San Francisco (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 65, 82.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint Of Plaintiff Quality International
Off Calendar Aug-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAEMIN CHANG OF FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP, TEL. NO. 364-5540 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SARIT SIMHAYOF COHEN, TEL. NO. 407-4197 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF QUALITY INTERNATIONAL PACKAGING, INC'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint is granted in part. Plaintiff has leave to add causes of action 3 through 7 against defendant World of Charms, Inc. and Sasimu LLC, Simayof, Inc., and Simus Inc. The court denies the motion as to Sarit Simayof-Cohen, Adi Simayof, and Yedidia Simayof. Defendant's evidence indicates that plaintiff knew or should have known of their potential liability earlier and allowing amendment a mere 4 days before the trial date would be prejudicial. In addition, if plaintiff recovers against defendant(s), plaintiff can bring a post judgment motion pursuant to CCP section 187 to add the individuals as additional alter ego judgment debtors. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047 =302/mjm
